last_item: Band-1: close the read-only CREATE-via-PUBLIC residual (internal/pg/provisionSQL)
last_result: done — provisionSQL now REVOKE CREATE ON SCHEMA public FROM PUBLIC + GRANT CREATE ON SCHEMA public TO indiepg_admin (scoped to the panel-managed `postgres` DB only; app DBs untouched by design). This strips the PUBLIC-inherited CREATE the per-role REVOKE couldn't, closing the last DB-level write vector for indiepg_readonly even with its GUC off. USAGE left intact (SELECT path preserved). Extended TestReadOnlyRole_DBLevelWriteDenial to assert read-only CREATE TABLE → 42501 with GUC off, and admin CREATE still works in postgres; proven green vs a throwaway PG14 cluster and non-vacuous (old SQL → read-only CREATE succeeds). Reviewer caught a real regression: the ALTER (re-provision) branch had dropped NOINHERIT → a second Provision silently left the role INHERIT; restored it + unit assertion, confirmed rolinherit=f after double-provision.
notes: Remaining band-1: secrets-at-rest (state DB file 0600 + a log-scrubbing test that S3 creds/Pushover tokens/admin hash are never logged); login brute-force lockout (rate-limit/lockout after N fails, verify throttle is end-to-end tested via the login handler). Then band 1.5 (data durability: last-good-backup surfacing, loud alert on stale/failed backup, S3-as-default nudge, restore-test surfacing). Integration tests are `//go:build integration`, skip unless INDIEPG_TEST_SOCKET set, and do NOT run in the normal gate. To prove DB-level role behavior locally: throwaway cluster via /usr/lib/postgresql/14/bin/{initdb,pg_ctl}, apply provisionSQL stmts via psql, point INDIEPG_TEST_SOCKET at the socket dir. go/psql/pg_ctl are snap-sandboxed (run with sandbox disabled). TMPDIR is empty in this shell — use /tmp explicitly.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
